headline.text: L1 S3 scraper dropped into nut-allergen dark chocolate buffer — *retrieved* by maintenance, no product shipped.

summary: One foreign-material near-miss at L1 S3 (scraper retrieved from buffer). L1 S3 MD level-4 clean on hold pending QA inspection Monday — hard-to-reach areas did not meet visual standard after first post-production deep clean. L3 ML4 scrap underreported by 526 lbs for second time this month.

food_safety.title: Blue scraper dropped into AD4 nut-allergen buffer
food_safety.summary: Employee dropped a blue scraper into the AD4 (nut allergen dark) buffer while scrubbing during level-4 MD clean; maintenance retrieved it before any product contact.
food_safety.status: Resolved — scraper fished out by maintenance at 21:22, no product exposed.

quality.title: Pre-zipped pouches failing to open on all 3 Matrix machines
quality.summary: Same lot (41021) of pouches arriving pre-zipped across Matrix 6, 8, and another unit; operators manually opening pouches to prevent product dumping onto sealed bags.
quality.status: Open — workaround in place (manual pouch opening), all on-hand pallets from same lot.

quality.title: ML4 scrap underreported by 526 lbs in SafetyChain
quality.summary: Third shift recorded 990 lbs scrap in SafetyChain but physical count was 1,516 lbs (chocolate, cream, caramel); system adjusted to match physical, second occurrence this month.
quality.status: Resolved — inventory adjusted, 3rd shift lead reminded by Rose Smuin.

sanitation.title: S3 MD level-4 clean incomplete, pending QA review Monday
sanitation.summary: First level-4 clean of MD (metal detector) after production run; inner rollers and center areas not accessible for visual clean even with 3 rollers removed — cleaning standard not met.
sanitation.status: On hold pending QA inspection Monday — team cleaned accessible areas, disassembly limits reached.

sanitation.title: S2 Tunnel 1 curtains not closed and taped
sanitation.summary: Tunnel 1 environmental curtains found open and not taped during shift — reminder issued that curtain control applies to all manufacturing, not just specific customers.
sanitation.status: Open — teams reminded to tape immediately, not days later.

opportunities.text: Consider adding MD inner-roller inspection ports or revised disassembly SOP to level-4 cleaning standard — today's first post-production deep clean could not visually verify center areas even with tension roller removed.
Consider incoming inspection or lot change-out for item 41021 pouches — all three Matrix machines saw pre-zipped failures from the same supplier lot.
Review scrap data-entry training and checklist for ML4 night shift — 526 lb underreport is the second gap this month per Rose Smuin.
